sectionName: Chemical & Physical Foundations of Biological Systems

questions.passage: An optics bench used a converging lens with focal length +10 cm. An object was placed at 30 cm, 20 cm, and 15 cm from the lens, and sharp real images were formed on a screen at approximately 15 cm, 20 cm, and 30 cm on the opposite side. Students used the thin-lens equation to relate object distance, image distance, and magnification.
questions.stem: When the object is 30 cm from the lens, where is the image formed?

choices.D: 15 cm on the opposite side of the lens
choices.A: 30 cm on the same side as the object

explanations.D: Correct. The passage and figure show that do = 30 cm gives di ≈ 15 cm. The thin-lens equation also gives 1/10 = 1/30 + 1/di, so 1/di = 1/15 and di = 15 cm.
explanations.A: A real image for a converging lens is on the opposite side, not the object side.
explanations.B: 10 cm is the focal length, not the image distance for this object placement.
explanations.C: An image at infinity occurs when the object is at the focal length, not 30 cm away.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Image distance measured for a converging lens with f = +10 cm.

questions.stem: What is the magnification when do = 30 cm and di = 15 cm?

explanations.C: Correct. Magnification is m = −di/do = −15/30 = −0.50. The negative sign indicates an inverted real image, and the magnitude indicates the image is half the object size.
explanations.A: Positive magnification would indicate an upright image, not the real inverted image here.
explanations.B: −2.0 would result from reversing the distance ratio.
explanations.D: +2.0 has both the wrong sign and the wrong magnitude.

questions.stem: When do equals 20 cm, why does di also equal 20 cm?

choices.B: The object is at twice the focal length
choices.A: The object is at the focal point

explanations.B: Correct. For a converging lens, an object at 2f forms a real image at 2f with equal size and inverted orientation. Here f = 10 cm, so 2f = 20 cm.
explanations.A: At the focal point, outgoing rays are parallel and the image forms at infinity.
explanations.C: The lens type does not change during the experiment.
explanations.D: A positive image distance on the opposite side indicates a real image.

questions.stem: If the object were moved to 5 cm from the lens, the image would be:

choices.B: Real and inverted on a screen

explanations.A: Correct. An object inside the focal length of a converging lens produces diverging refracted rays that appear to originate from the object side. The image is virtual, upright, and cannot be projected on a screen.
explanations.B: A real inverted image occurs only when the object is outside the focal length.
explanations.C: The thin-lens equation would give a negative image distance, not a real 5 cm screen image.
explanations.D: Light still refracts; the image type changes.

questions.stem: Which equation was used to analyze the lens?

explanations.D: Correct. The thin-lens equation relates focal length, object distance, and image distance as 1/f = 1/do + 1/di. It explains the reciprocal relationship plotted in the passage.
explanations.A: Focal length is not simply the sum of object and image distances.
explanations.B: Magnification involves −di/do, but that is not the lens equation itself.
explanations.C: P = ρgh is the hydrostatic pressure equation, not an optics equation.

questions.passage: A Doppler ultrasound probe emitted sound at 2.00 MHz toward moving blood. Reflected frequency shifts were measured at three flow speeds: 0.20, 0.40, and 0.60 m/s. The measured frequency shifts were approximately proportional to speed. Students assumed the speed of sound in tissue was 1540 m/s and that the angle between the beam and flow was held constant.
questions.stem: What relationship is shown between flow speed and Doppler shift?

choices.C: The shift increases approximately linearly with speed
choices.A: The shift decreases as speed increases
choices.B: The shift is independent of speed
choices.D: The shift is largest at zero speed

explanations.C: Correct. The plotted shifts double when speed doubles from 0.20 to 0.40 m/s and triple when speed rises to 0.60 m/s. That pattern is approximately linear under fixed angle and sound frequency.
explanations.A: The data show increasing, not decreasing, shifts.
explanations.B: The shift clearly changes with speed.
explanations.D: A stationary reflector would not produce the largest Doppler shift.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 2. Doppler frequency shift measured at increasing blood speeds.

questions.stem: Why does Doppler ultrasound provide information about blood motion?

choices.B: Moving scatterers reflect sound with a shifted frequency
choices.A: Sound intensity becomes independent of distance

explanations.B: Correct. The Doppler effect occurs when relative motion changes the observed frequency of a wave. Moving blood cells act as scatterers, so reflected ultrasound has a frequency shift related to blood velocity.
explanations.A: Intensity-distance relationships are separate from Doppler frequency shifts.
explanations.C: Ultrasound is nonionizing mechanical sound, not ionizing radiation.
explanations.D: Absorption is not the mechanism that creates the velocity-dependent frequency shift.

questions.stem: If the angle between beam and flow increased toward 90°, the measured Doppler shift would:

explanations.A: Correct. Doppler ultrasound depends on the velocity component along the beam, proportional to cosθ. As θ approaches 90°, cosθ approaches zero, so the measured frequency shift decreases toward zero.
explanations.B: The axial velocity component does not increase without limit as angle approaches 90°.
explanations.C: The shift depends on angle, so it would not remain unchanged.
explanations.D: Changing beam angle does not convert the measurement into hydrostatic pressure.

questions.stem: The emitted ultrasound frequency of 2.00 MHz is best described as:

choices.D: Above the range of human hearing

explanations.D: Correct. Human hearing typically extends to about 20 kHz. A frequency of 2.00 MHz is far higher, so it is ultrasound and cannot be heard by humans.
explanations.A: Infrasound is below human hearing, not millions of hertz.
explanations.B: Ultrasound is a mechanical wave, not visible electromagnetic radiation.

questions.stem: Which assumption is needed to compare the shifts across speeds?

choices.A: The blood speed was zero in all trials
choices.D: The speed of sound in tissue was negative

explanations.C: Correct. Because Doppler shift depends on both speed and the cosine of the beam-flow angle, holding the angle constant allows the observed changes in shift to be attributed to speed changes.
explanations.A: Zero speed would eliminate the velocity differences being studied.
explanations.B: Random probe-frequency changes would confound the comparison.
explanations.D: Sound speed is positive and was treated as constant.
